questions.text: Laravel Sanctum SPA authentication configuration best practices: session cookie settings (SameSite, Secure, domain), CSRF protection with Vue 3, remember_token handling for 30-day remember me
Laravel built-in email verification implementation: MustVerifyEmail interface, signed URL generation with FRONTEND_URL, verification routes, resend verification throttle configuration
Laravel built-in password reset flow: token generation, 60-minute expiration, remember_token invalidation on password reset, secure token storage, reset email templates
Laravel Sanctum session persistence: remember_token (60 chars), cookie lifetime 30 days, session regeneration on login/logout, secure flag in production, Redis session driver configuration

Vue 3 + Pinia authentication store patterns: state management for user/isAuthenticated/loading, actions for login/register/logout/fetchUser/checkAuth, axios interceptors for 401/403/422 handling, XSRF-TOKEN cookie reading, router guards (requiresAuth, requiresVerified, roles)
Laravel rate limiting for auth endpoints: throttle middleware configuration, per-IP limits (5 req/min), custom key resolver for auth endpoints, Redis-backed rate limiter

Laravel security hardening for SPA auth: secure headers, SameSite=Lax vs Strict, HttpOnly cookies, CSRF token rotation, bcrypt rounds configuration, secure password reset tokens, Sanctum CSRF protection
